user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Scheduled Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected initiated for Northwind Traders.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-24 17:37 EST
Subject: Re: Re: forecast prep Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with forecast prep. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
Date: 2024-01-07 14:16 EST
Subject: Re: Re: forecast prep Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about forecast prep. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Northwind Traders
Date: 2024-02-22
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Kevin (Northwind Traders)

[00:02:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on forecast prep from our last session.

[00:07:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:14:00] Kevin (Northwind Traders): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:16:00] Kevin (Northwind Traders): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:25:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:29:00] Kevin (Northwind Traders): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:32:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:34:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.
